repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lingam_direction_min_order_v1 — generic non-Gaussian cumulant recovery, not SCORE geometry

published_prior_art.cite: rolland2022score — known SCORE leaf characterization; the mirror local pair is the incremental addition
zhu2023sample supplies a SCORE upper guarantee; no verified paper derives a Chernoff-to-score-gap local expansion at the Gaussian reversible center
chernoff1952measure supplies the generic simple-testing exponent, but not this ANM subexperiment or its proportionality to C_m
hoyer2008nonlinear — linear-Gaussian additive models are the reversible exceptional case
koehler2023efficiency concerns density-estimation efficiency, not a SCORE causal-order error exponent

structure_flags.label: Theorem 2 / project headline
structure_flags.one_line: The strongest open kernel is labelled a theorem although it is intentionally unproved; reclassify the headline frontier as a conjecture.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 and comparator_promise_table
novelty_flags.one_line: Calling the binary-subexperiment converse a strict tightening of zhu2023sample is unsupported: no statement gives a strict comparison to Zhu's SCORE upper bound, and the theorem concerns an optimal likelihood rule.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand and estimand_functional
soundness_flags.one_line: o(P) is not an observed-law functional at P_0, where the identical linear-Gaussian law has both arrow representations; restrict its domain to delta != 0 or make h the structural testing label.

soundness_flags.label: C_star(delta)
soundness_flags.one_line: Its integral uses dz although p_{h,delta} is a density on R^2; specify integration over R^2 with respect to dx dy.

soundness_flags.label: ass:local-integrability
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated exponential-integrability condition is not a standard QMD condition attributable to chernoff1952measure; Chernoff's testing result does not establish that named assumption.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / honest_scope
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The claimed sharp exponent is only for the explicitly constructed two-node Gaussian ANM subexperiment, not a global nonlinear-ANM minimax theorem or a feasible score implementation.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Sharp likelihood benchmark conditional on the mirror-symmetric two-point subexperiment and its stated regularity assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace every “strict_tightening” promise for Zhu, Hoyer, and Peters with “informed by,” unless a new statement gives an explicit shared-margin inequality or strict sample-complexity comparison to the named result.
Define the target as h on the indexed experiment for all delta, while reserving o(P) as an observed-law functional only on the non-linear identifiable subset; also correct the R^2 Chernoff integral.

Retag local integrability as a bespoke sufficient regularity condition with a precise derivation, or replace it by verifiable QMD/Chernoff regularity conditions that admit the advertised mechanism family.
Add Kpotufe et al. (2014) as a finite-sample ANM-direction comparator and explicitly explain why its consistency analysis is orthogonal to the local SCORE/Chernoff modulus.

recommended_next_step: Revise this angle: relabel the main frontier as a conjecture, repair the two well-posedness defects and regularity citation, and remove unsupported strict-comparator language before advancing.
